question_title: Will the official result of the Lamag by-election (Sabah), scheduled for January 24, 2026, be overturned before May 1, 2026?
summary: The Lamag by-election on January 24, 2026 resulted in a BN landslide: Mohd Ismail Ayob won with 7,269 votes vs Warisan's 1,588 (5,681-vote majority). No election petition or legal challenge has been filed, and the 21-day filing deadline under Malaysian law has likely passed. The election was conducted smoothly with only minor infractions. Even in the extraordinary scenario of annulment, a re-run would almost certainly produce the same winner given the margin. There is no credible path to YES resolution.

factors.description: BN won by a massive 5,681-vote majority (7,269 vs 1,588) - no plausible irregularity could change the outcome
No election petition or legal challenge filed against the by-election result - extensive multi-source search found nothing
21-day petition filing deadline under Malaysian law has likely passed (~Feb 14, 2026) with no petition filed
Election conducted smoothly - only 5 minor offences reported (campaign materials, provocation)
No recent precedent for Malaysian by-election results being overturned by courts
Resolution requires not just annulment but change in winning party - implausible given landslide margin even in re-run scenario

reasoning:
**Forecast: 1% (NO)**

The Lamag by-election result will almost certainly not be overturned before May 1, 2026. Key reasons:

- **Landslide result**: BN's Mohd Ismail won with 7,269 votes vs 1,588 — a 5,681-vote majority that makes any irregularity-based challenge moot
- **No legal challenge filed**: Multiple search angles across Malaysian news sources found zero evidence of any petition or court filing
- **Filing deadline passed**: The ~21-day statutory deadline for election petitions elapsed around February 14 with no action
- **Clean election**: Only 5 minor infractions reported (campaign materials, one provocation incident)
- **High bar for resolution**: Even annulment wouldn't suffice — the question requires a re-run that *changes the winning party*, which is implausible given the margin
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) Will the official result of the Lamag by-election (Sabah), scheduled for January 24, 2026, be overturned before May 1, 2026?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/42118)
